text: It feels strange to express my inability to sit while surrounded by others who are so focused and settled, absorbed in their seats and in the event unfolding before us. There is a subtle discomfort in not being able to share in the simple act of sitting, especially here, where attention and stillness seem woven into the atmosphere. Yet, I am grateful for the invitation, and I find a certain freedom in my restlessness—a reminder that participation can take many forms. The world continues to invite me in, even when I must remain on the fringes, moving or standing, fully awake to the present moment's nuance.
